description_long: This is a roughly hewn, rectangular stone block with a predominantly off-white coloration and subtle yellowish and gray veining throughout its surface. The block features two old, yellowed paper labels affixed to one of its larger faces, with handwritten text indicating mineralogical information. The stone's texture appears coarse and unpolished, with visible natural fractures and irregularities along its edges and faces. The block is solid and dense, likely composed of feldspar or a similar mineral, as suggested by the label. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a physical barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements aside from the labels.
description: A coarse, off-white stone block with yellowish and gray veining, featuring two old paper labels with handwritten text. The block is solid, irregular, and dense, with a rough, unpolished surface.
description_view_0: This view shows the largest face of the stone block, prominently displaying two yellowed paper labels with handwritten text and the most visible veining.
description_view_1: This view presents a more cubic end of the block, with minimal visible labeling and a focus on the stone's rough, uneven texture.
description_view_2: This side view highlights the block's elongated shape, the slanted top face, and the subtle color variations without showing the labels clearly.
description_view_3: This view looks down onto the block, showing the top label in detail and the block's rectangular cross-section, with some surface irregularities visible.
attribute_location_context: museum display or educational mineral collection
attribute_installation_method: placed directly on a flat surface or shelf
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface is rough but appears unexposed to outdoor elements, with no moss or erosion.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Two old paper labels with handwritten text are affixed to the stone's surface.
